rationale: Low-backlash HP variants in the 60/90 frame match cobot joint envelopes. Smooth output and modest weight are ideal.
HT variants handle continuous-duty loads, but a Rollsate roller screw or KGV right-angle unit is usually a better wheel-drive fit.
Peak torque on 90/140 frame HT variants comfortably handles index-and-hold cycles; backlash is acceptable for station-aligned dials.
HP variants meet the low-backlash requirement; for the tightest precision axes, pair with a direct-encoder feedback loop.
Possible but not optimal — a SpiralBevel or KGV right-angle unit usually wins on self-locking behavior and form factor for tracker mounts.
HP variants give the smooth low-speed control needed; ensure outdoor enclosure/sealing requirements are evaluated.
